lines.translation: Zhang Tianming ist ein Studienanfänger. Das Semester beginnt bald. Sein Zuhause ist sehr weit von der Universität entfernt, deshalb muss er mit dem Flugzeug zur Uni fliegen. Der Flug dauerte über zwei Stunden. Nach der Landung rief er sofort ein Taxi und kam schnell im Studentenwohnheim an.
Hier sind ja so viele Leute!
Du bist doch ein Studienanfänger, oder?
Ja, ich bin ein Studienanfänger. Und du?
Ich bin Masterstudent. Ich helfe hier den Erstsemestern beim Einzug. Wie heißt du?
Ich heiße Zhang Tianming.
Zhang Tianming? Ist das ein chinesischer Name?
Ja, meine Eltern kommen aus China. Aber ich bin in den USA geboren und aufgewachsen. Und dein Name ist...?
Ich studiere Chinesisch. Mein chinesischer Name ist Ke Lin. Welche drei Schriftzeichen hat dein Name?
Zhang ist das Zhang von 'gong-chang' [zh:工厂], das 'zhang' wie in 'ein Blatt Papier'. Tian ist das Tian wie in 'Wetter', und Ming ist das Ming wie in 'morgen'.
Wie bist du zur Uni gekommen?
Ich bin zuerst geflogen und habe dann ein Taxi vom Flughafen zur Uni genommen. Ke Lin, wohnst du auch hier?
Nein, das ist das Erstsemester-Wohnheim. Ich wohne außerhalb des Campus.
Ach so? Warum wohnst du außerhalb des Campus? Glaubst du, es ist besser, auf oder außerhalb des Campus zu wohnen?

Manche Leute wohnen gerne auf dem Campus, weil sie es für praktisch und sicher halten. Andere wohnen lieber außerhalb des Campus, da Wohnungen außerhalb des Campus relativ günstig sind. Ich wohne außerhalb des Campus. Ich möchte nicht nur Geld sparen, sondern auch Freiheit haben. Außerdem ist das Wohnen auf dem Campus nicht unbedingt so praktisch.
Echt? Dann ziehe ich später auch außerhalb des Campus.

Du bist gerade erst angekommen. Es ist gut für dich, auf dem Campus zu wohnen, um dich an das Studentenleben zu gewöhnen. Wenn du später umziehen möchtest, kann ich dir helfen, eine Wohnung zu finden.
Okay. Wenn ich in Zukunft umziehe, werde ich dich auf jeden Fall um Hilfe bitten.
Tianming, der Weg ist frei. Lass mich dir helfen, das Gepäck reinzutragen.
Okay, danke. Hey, wo ist mein Computer? ... Oh nein! Ich habe den Computer wohl im Taxi vergessen!

language_notes.label: a
language_notes.explanation_en: 新生 (xīnshēng) (wörtl. neuer Student) ist der chinesische Begriff für „Erstsemester“. Der Begriff 新鲜人 (xīnxiānrén), eine vielleicht ursprünglich scherzhafte Übersetzung des Begriffs „Erstsemester“, wird in Taiwan verwendet. Das Adjektiv 新鲜 (xīnxian) (frisch) beschreibt jedoch typischerweise Lebensmittel oder Dinge, die neuartig oder ungewöhnlich sind. Wiederkehrende Studenten werden 老生 (lǎoshēng) (wörtl. alter Student) genannt.

language_notes.label: b
language_notes.explanation_en: Das Gegenteil von 好处 (hǎochu) (Vorteil, Nutzen) ist 坏处 (huàichu) (Nachteil, Schaden).

language_notes.label: c
language_notes.explanation_en: 拉 (là) wird hier in seiner umgangssprachlichen Bedeutung „etwas vergessen/liegen lassen“ verwendet. 拉 wird ansonsten „lā“ ausgesprochen und bedeutet „ziehen“. Manche Leute bevorzugen die Verwendung des Zeichens 落, das normalerweise „luò“ ausgesprochen wird und „fallen“ bedeutet.

grammar_sections.title: Die Partikel 了 (IV)
grammar_sections.explanation_en: Die dynamische Partikel 了 kann anzeigen, dass eine Handlung stattgefunden hat. Sie kann nach einem Verb stehen; zwischen einem Verb und einem quantifizierten Objekt, wie in (A) und (B); oder nach einem unquantifizierten Objekt am Satzende, wie in (C). In allen obigen Beispielen signalisiert 了, dass eine Handlung stattgefunden hat, aber das Hinzufügen von 了 ist nicht gleichbedeutend mit der Bildung einer Vergangenheitsform des Verbs. In einigen Fällen zeigt 了 an, dass eine Handlung tatsächlich in der Zukunft stattfinden wird. Sätze mit 了, die das Stattfinden einer Handlung anzeigen, haben normalerweise eine Zeitangabe, wie in (A), (B) und (C). Wenn der Satz keine Zeitangabe enthält, ist die implizierte Zeit die unmittelbare Vergangenheit. Wenn 了 zwischen einem Verb und einem Objekt steht, ist das Objekt normalerweise quantifiziert, wie in (A) und (B), muss es aber nicht sein, wenn eine von drei Umständen zutrifft: wenn dem Objekt ein weiteres 了 folgt, wie in (G); wenn dem Objekt ein weiterer Satzteil folgt, wie in (H); oder wenn sich das Objekt auf eine bestimmte Person oder Sache bezieht, wie in (I). Wenn 了 am Satzende steht, kann es das Eintreten oder die Realisierung eines Ereignisses, Zustands oder einer neuen Situation bedeuten. Wenn es in einem Satz zwei Verbphrasen gibt und der ersten Verbphrase die Partikel 了 folgt, sind die beiden durch die Verben bezeichneten Handlungen aufeinanderfolgend. Die zweite Handlung beginnt, sobald die erste abgeschlossen ist.

patterns.meaning_en: Zeigt eine abgeschlossene Handlung mit einem quantifizierten Objekt an.
Zeigt eine abgeschlossene Handlung an, wenn das Objekt verstanden oder nicht vorhanden ist.
Zeigt eine abgeschlossene Handlung an, oft mit einem unquantifizierten Objekt.

patterns.pattern: Verb Phrase 1 + 了 + ... + 就 + Verb Phrase 2 + 了
patterns.meaning_en: Zeigt zwei aufeinanderfolgende Handlungen an, wobei die zweite direkt nach der ersten stattfindet.
Zeigt eine neue Situation oder eine Zustandsänderung an.

examples.hanzi: 昨天晚上我看了一个电影。
examples.translation: Letzte Nacht habe ich einen Film gesehen.

examples.hanzi: 小王刚才在网上买了两本书。

examples.hanzi: Q: 这本书你看了吗? A: 我看了。

examples.hanzi: Q: 你买明天的电影票了吗? A: 买了。

examples.hanzi: 我想今天晚上看电影,可是明天要考试,所以不看了。

examples.hanzi: 昨天我搬进了宿舍就去餐厅吃饭了。

usage_notes: 了 signalisiert, dass eine Handlung stattgefunden hat, aber das Hinzufügen von 了 ist nicht gleichbedeutend mit der Bildung einer Vergangenheitsform des Verbs. In einigen Fällen zeigt 了 an, dass eine Handlung tatsächlich in der Zukunft stattfinden wird.
Sätze mit 了, die das Eintreten einer Handlung anzeigen, enthalten normalerweise eine Zeitangabe. Wenn der Satz keine Zeitangabe enthält, ist die implizierte Zeit die unmittelbare Vergangenheit.

Wenn 了 zwischen einem Verb und einem Objekt erscheint, ist das Objekt normalerweise quantifiziert. Dies ist jedoch nicht notwendig, wenn eine von drei Umständen zutrifft: wenn dem Objekt ein weiteres 了 folgt; wenn dem Objekt ein weiterer Nebensatz folgt; oder wenn sich das Objekt auf eine bestimmte Person oder Sache bezieht.
Wenn 了 am Ende eines Satzes steht, kann es das Eintreten oder die Realisierung eines Ereignisses, Zustands oder einer neuen Situation bedeuten.

Wenn es in einem Satz zwei Verbphrasen gibt und die erste Verbphrase von der Partikel 了 gefolgt wird, sind die beiden durch die Verben bezeichneten Handlungen aufeinanderfolgend. Die zweite Handlung beginnt, sobald die erste abgeschlossen ist.

grammar_sections.title: Sätze mit 是…的 (I) – Wiederholung
grammar_sections.explanation_en: Wir haben diese Konstruktion in IC2 gelernt, siehe Grammatik 4, Lektion 14, IC2. Denken Sie daran: Wenn sowohl der Sprecher als auch der Zuhörer wissen, dass eine Handlung oder ein Ereignis stattgefunden hat, und der Sprecher die Besonderheiten der Handlung hervorheben möchte – wie Zeit, Ort, Art und Weise, Zweck oder Handelnden –, muss die 是…的-Konstruktion verwendet werden. Beachten Sie, dass in den folgenden Beispielen die Partikel 了 verwendet wird, um zu fragen, ob eine Handlung stattgefunden hat. Sobald dies bestätigt ist, wechseln die Sprecher sofort zur 是…的-Konstruktion, um die Einzelheiten der Handlung zu erfragen. Beachten Sie auch, dass, obwohl wir sie die 是…的-Konstruktion nennen, 是 tatsächlich oft optional ist.

patterns.meaning_en: Wird verwendet, um nach der Zeit, dem Ort, der Art und Weise, dem Zweck oder dem Handelnden einer vergangenen Handlung zu fragen oder diese zu betonen, von der bereits bekannt ist, dass sie stattgefunden hat.

usage_notes: Wenn nach dem Verb ein Objekt steht, kann 的 am Ende des Satzes oder vor dem Objekt platziert werden.
In verneinenden Sätzen kann 是 nicht weggelassen werden.

grammar_sections.title: 除了…以外, 都…
grammar_sections.explanation_en: „除了 [zh:chúle]“ bedeutet „nicht mitgezählt“. Zur Wiederholung: Die Struktur „除了…以外, 还/也…“ [zh:chúle...yǐwài, hái/yě...] führt etwas ein, das zusätzlich zu dem bereits Bekannten existiert. Andererseits betont die Struktur „除了…以外, 都…“ [zh:chúle...yǐwài, dōu...], dass eine Gruppe von Personen oder Dingen etwas gemeinsam hat, wobei die genannten Ausnahmen ausgeschlossen sind. Sie wird normalerweise mit „außer“ oder „außer für“ übersetzt.

examples.hanzi: 他除了学中文以外,还学日文。
examples.pinyin: Tā chúle xué Zhōngwén yǐwài, hái xué Rìwén.
examples.translation: Außer Chinesisch lernt er auch Japanisch. Das heißt: Er lernt Chinesisch und auch Japanisch.

examples.hanzi: 我们班除了小王以外,还有小林去过中国。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen bān chúle Xiǎo Wáng yǐwài, hái yǒu Xiǎo Lín qùguo Zhōngguó.
examples.translation: In unserer Klasse war außer Klein Wang auch Klein Lin in China. Das heißt: Klein Wang und Klein Lin waren beide in China.

examples.hanzi: 昨天张天明除了搬家以外,还买东西了。
examples.pinyin: Zuótiān Zhāng Tiānmíng chúle bānjiā yǐwài, hái mǎi dōngxi le.
examples.translation: Außer umzuziehen, ist Zhang Tianming gestern auch einkaufen gegangen. Das heißt: Gestern ist Zhang Tianming umgezogen und einkaufen gegangen.

examples.hanzi: 除了小柯以外,我们班的同学都去过中国。
examples.pinyin: Chúle Xiǎo Kē yǐwài, wǒmen bān de tóngxué dōu qùguo Zhōngguó.
examples.translation: Außer Klein Ke war jeder Schüler in unserer Klasse in China. Das heißt: Klein Ke ist der Einzige in unserer Klasse, der nicht in China war.

examples.hanzi: 除了看书以外,晚上什么事我都愿意做。
examples.pinyin: Chúle kànshū yǐwài, wǎnshang shénme shì wǒ dōu yuànyì zuò.
examples.translation: Außer Lesen bin ich abends zu allem bereit. Das heißt: Lesen ist das Einzige, was ich abends nicht tun möchte.

grammar_sections.title: Sätze verbinden (I)
grammar_sections.explanation_en: Für einen guten Redefluss und Klarheit sind Bindewörter notwendig, wenn man ausführlich in mehreren Sätzen spricht. Bindewörter wie 因为 [zh:yīnwèi] (weil), 除了…以外 [zh:chúle...yǐwài] (zusätzlich zu), 还因为 [zh:hái yīnwèi] (auch weil) und 再说 [zh:zàishuō] (obendrein) können verwendet werden, um Sätze zu strukturieren. Eine weitere Möglichkeit, Sätze zu verbinden, ist die Verwendung von Ordnungszahlen, um Gründe aufzuzählen: 第一… [zh:dìyī...] (erstens), 第二… [zh:dì'èr...] (zweitens), 第三… [zh:dìsān...] (drittens). Diese Bindewörter lassen eine Antwort klarer und strukturierter klingen.

patterns.pattern: 因为..., 除了...以外, 还因为..., 再说...
patterns.meaning_en: Eine Reihe von Bindewörtern, die verwendet werden, um mehrere Gründe für eine Handlung oder Meinung aufzulisten, und die bedeuten: „weil..., zusätzlich zu..., auch weil..., obendrein...“.

patterns.pattern_pinyin: dì yī..., dì èr..., dì sān...
patterns.meaning_en: Eine Struktur, die Ordnungszahlen verwendet, um Gründe oder Punkte der Reihe nach aufzuzählen, und die bedeutet: „erstens..., zweitens..., drittens...“.

examples.hanzi: 有的人喜欢住在学校宿舍，觉得又方便又安全，有的人喜欢住在校外，因为校外的房子比较便宜。我住在校外，除了想省点儿钱以外，还因为住在校外自由。再说，住在校内也不见得很方便。
examples.pinyin: Yǒude rén xǐhuan zhù zài xuéxiào sùshè, juéde yòu fāngbiàn yòu ānquán, yǒude rén xǐhuan zhù zài xiào wài, yīnwèi xiào wài de fángzi bǐjiào piányi. Wǒ zhù zài xiào wài, chúle xiǎng shěng diǎnr qián yǐwài, hái yīnwèi zhù zài xiào wài zìyóu. Zàishuō, zhù zài xiào nèi yě bújiànde hěn fāngbiàn.
examples.translation: Manche Leute wohnen gerne auf dem Campus, weil sie es für praktisch und sicher halten. Andere wohnen gerne außerhalb des Campus, da Wohnungen außerhalb des Campus relativ günstig sind. Ich wohne außerhalb des Campus. Außer dass ich etwas Geld sparen möchte, will ich auch Freiheit. Obendrein ist das Wohnen auf dem Campus nicht unbedingt so praktisch.

examples.hanzi: 有的人喜欢住在学校宿舍，觉得又方便又安全，有的人喜欢住在校外，因为，第一，校外的房子比较便宜，第二，住在校外比较自由，第三，住在校内不见得很方便。
examples.translation: Manche Leute wohnen gerne in den Studentenwohnheimen, weil sie es für praktisch und sicher halten. Manche Leute wohnen gerne außerhalb des Campus, weil erstens Wohnungen außerhalb des Campus günstiger sind, zweitens das Wohnen außerhalb des Campus mehr Freiheit bietet und drittens das Wohnen auf dem Campus nicht unbedingt sehr praktisch ist.

global_config.visual_style: Cinematic, realistic, bright and sunny day, modern American university campus setting.
global_config.setting_summary_cn: 大学一年级新生张天明，在开学第一天从家出发，乘飞机、坐出租车，最终抵达热闹的学校宿舍区，并在这里遇到了前来帮忙的研究生学长柯林。
global_config.setting_summary_en: On the first day of school, college freshman Zhang Tianming travels from home by plane and taxi, finally arriving at the bustling university dormitory area, where he meets Ke Lin, a helpful graduate student.

character_definitions.name: 张天明
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A young Asian male, around 18 years old, with short black hair. He looks excited and a bit overwhelmed. Dressed in casual travel clothes (hoodie, jeans), pulling a large, heavy suitcase.

character_definitions.name: 柯林
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A slightly older male, around 22 years old, Caucasian, with a friendly and approachable demeanor. Wearing a university T-shirt and shorts, looking helpful and confident.

hanzi: 张天明是大学一年级的新生。快开学了,他家离大学很远,得坐飞机去学校。他坐飞机坐了两个多小时,下飞机以后,马上叫了一辆出租汽车,很快就到了学校宿舍。
pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng shì dàxué yī niánjí de xīnshēng kuài kāixué le tā jiā lí dàxué hěn yuǎn děi zuò fēijī qù xuéxiào tā zuò fēijī zuò le liǎng ge duō xiǎoshí xià fēijī yǐhòu mǎshàng jiào le yí liàng chūzū qìchē hěn kuài jiù dào le xuéxiào sùshè
translation: Zhang Tianming is a college freshman. School is starting soon. His home is very far from the university, so he has to take a plane to school. He was on the plane for more than two hours. After getting off the plane, he immediately hailed a taxi and quickly arrived at the school dorm.

scenes.scene_goal: 展现张天明长途跋涉来到学校的过程。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 一组快速的蒙太奇镜头：张天明在飞机上望向窗外，下飞机后穿过人流，在机场外招手叫出租车，最后出租车缓缓驶入大学校门，停在宿舍楼前。
scenes.setting_en: Montage: Inside an airplane, airport terminal, outside the airport, and arriving at a university campus.
scenes.shot_type: Medium shot, wide shot, point-of-view shot.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Vibrant montage sequence: 1. Medium shot of Zhang Tianming, a young Asian student, looking out an airplane window at the clouds. 2. He walks through a busy airport terminal, pulling a large suitcase. 3. Wide shot of him hailing a yellow taxi outside the airport. 4. The taxi drives through the main gate of a beautiful, green university campus and pulls up to a modern dorm building with a 'Welcome Freshmen' banner.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 张天明是大学一年级的新生。快开学了,他家离大学很远,得坐飞机去学校。他坐飞机坐了两个多小时,下飞机以后,马上叫了一辆出租汽车,很快就到了学校宿舍。
voice_over.pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng shì dàxué yī niánjí de xīnshēng kuài kāixué le tā jiā lí dàxué hěn yuǎn děi zuò fēijī qù xuéxiào tā zuò fēijī zuò le liǎng ge duō xiǎoshí xià fēijī yǐhòu mǎshàng jiào le yí liàng chūzū qìchē hěn kuài jiù dào le xuéxiào sùshè
voice_over.translation: Zhang Tianming is a college freshman. School is starting soon. His home is very far from the university, so he has to take a plane to school. He was on the plane for more than two hours. After getting off the plane, he immediately hailed a taxi and quickly arrived at the school dorm.

scenes.scene_goal: 表现张天明初到校园，被热闹的迎新场面所震撼。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 张天明从出租车上下来，拖着沉重的行李箱，站在路边。他环顾四周，看到校园里人来人往，到处是新生和家长，非常热闹。他不禁小声感叹。
scenes.setting_en: In front of a university dormitory on a sunny day. The area is crowded with students, parents, and moving boxes.
scenes.camera_movement: Slow pan following his gaze, then a slight push-in on his face.
scenes.shot_type: Wide shot from his POV, then a medium close-up.
scenes.video_prompt_en: A yellow taxi drives away, leaving Zhang Tianming standing on the curb with his large suitcase. A wide point-of-view shot shows a bustling campus move-in day: students laughing, parents helping with boxes, colorful banners. The camera cuts to a medium close-up of Zhang Tianming's face, his eyes wide with awe as he takes it all in.

pinyin: shì wǒ shì xīnshēng nǐ ne
translation: Yes, I'm a new student. And you?

scenes.scene_goal: 引入新角色柯林，通过第一次对话建立联系。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 柯林看到独自站着的张天明，友好地走上前搭话。张天明有些惊讶，但还是礼貌地回答并反问。镜头在两人之间切换，表现初次见面的互动。
scenes.setting_en: In front of the university dormitory, amidst the crowd.
scenes.video_prompt_en: A friendly-looking older student, Ke Lin, notices Zhang Tianming and approaches him with a smile. Over-the-shoulder shot of Ke Lin asking, 'You're a new student, right?'. Reverse shot on Zhang Tianming, who nods and replies, 'Yes, I'm a new student. And you?', looking back at Ke Lin with curiosity.

hanzi: 我是研究生。在这儿帮新生搬东西。请问,你叫什么名字？
pinyin: wǒ shì yánjiūshēng zài zhèr bāng xīnshēng bān dōngxi qǐngwèn nǐ jiào shénme míngzi
translation: I'm a graduate student. I'm here helping the freshmen move their things. What's your name?

scenes.scene_goal: 双方进行自我介绍，柯林展现出友善助人的学长形象。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 柯林指了指周围忙碌的景象和自己身上的T恤，解释自己是来帮忙的研究生，然后自然地询问张天明的名字。张天明看着他，清楚地报上自己的名字。
scenes.setting_en: In front of the university dormitory.
scenes.shot_type: Medium shot of Ke Lin, then a close-up on Zhang Tianming.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium shot of Ke Lin. He gestures to the busy scene and his university T-shirt, explaining with a warm smile that he's a grad student volunteer. He then asks for Zhang Tianming's name. Cut to a close-up of Zhang Tianming's face as he replies clearly, 'My name is Zhang Tianming.'

voice_over.speaker: 柯林
voice_over.hanzi: 我是研究生。在这儿帮新生搬东西。请问,你叫什么名字？
voice_over.pinyin: wǒ shì yánjiūshēng zài zhèr bāng xīnshēng bān dōngxi qǐngwèn nǐ jiào shénme míngzi
voice_over.translation: I'm a graduate student. I'm here helping the freshmen move their things. What's your name?

hanzi: 真的吗？那我以后也搬到校外去。
pinyin: zhēn de ma nà wǒ yǐ hòu yě bān dào xiào wài qù
translation: Really? Then I'll move off campus in the future, too.

scenes.scene_goal: 张天明被住校外的想法吸引，表现出冲动和向往。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 特写镜头，张天明听完柯林的话，眼睛一亮，脸上露出向往和兴奋的表情，他立刻决定自己以后也要搬出去。
scenes.setting_en: Daytime, outside a university freshman dormitory. Zhang Tianming and Ke Lin are standing with luggage.
scenes.camera_movement: Static shot, focused on Zhang Tianming.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Close-up shot of a young Asian student (Zhang Tianming) outdoors on a university campus. His eyes light up with excitement and he looks convinced, speaking animatedly. His friend is partially visible in the background.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 真的吗？那我以后也搬到校外去。
voice_over.pinyin: zhēn de ma nà wǒ yǐ hòu yě bān dào xiào wài qù
voice_over.translation: Really? Then I'll move off campus in the future, too.

hanzi: 你刚来,在学校住对你有好处,可以适应一下学校的生活。要是你以后想搬家,我可以帮你找房子。
pinyin: nǐ gāng lái zài xué xiào zhù duì nǐ yǒu hǎo chu kě yǐ shì yìng yí xià xué xiào de shēng huó yào shi nǐ yǐ hòu xiǎng bān jiā wǒ kě yǐ bāng nǐ zhǎo fáng zi
translation: You've just arrived. It's good for you to live on campus to get used to school life. If you want to move later, I can help you find a place.

scenes.scene_goal: 柯林作为学长，给出理性和贴心的建议，展现其成熟稳重的一面。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头切换到柯林，他微笑着，像个大哥哥一样，拍了拍张天明的肩膀，耐心地劝说他先在校内住下适应环境。
scenes.setting_en: Daytime, outside a university freshman dormitory. Standing with Zhang Tianming and his luggage.
scenes.camera_movement: Static shot, focused on Ke Lin.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium close-up shot of a friendly American student (Ke Lin) on a university campus. He smiles warmly and gives advice to his younger friend, patting his shoulder reassuringly. The younger friend is visible, listening.

voice_over.speaker: 柯林
voice_over.hanzi: 你刚来,在学校住对你有好处,可以适应一下学校的生活。要是你以后想搬家,我可以帮你找房子。
voice_over.pinyin: nǐ gāng lái zài xué xiào zhù duì nǐ yǒu hǎo chu kě yǐ shì yìng yí xià xué xiào de shēng huó yào shi nǐ yǐ hòu xiǎng bān jiā wǒ kě yǐ bāng nǐ zhǎo fáng zi
voice_over.translation: You've just arrived. It's good for you to live on campus to get used to school life. If you want to move later, I can help you find a place.

hanzi: 好吧,我以后要是搬家,一定请你帮忙。
pinyin: hǎo ba wǒ yǐ hòu yào shi bān jiā yí dìng qǐng nǐ bāng máng
translation: OK. If I move in the future, I'll definitely ask for your help.

scenes.scene_goal: 张天明接受了柯林的建议，并表达了感谢和未来的请求。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头回到张天明，他听了柯林的话，感激地点了点头，诚恳地表示以后如果搬家一定会请他帮忙。
scenes.setting_en: Daytime, outside a university freshman dormitory. Standing with Ke Lin.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Close-up shot of the young Asian student (Zhang Tianming). He looks grateful and nods in agreement, speaking sincerely to his friend who is just out of frame.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 好吧,我以后要是搬家,一定请你帮忙。
voice_over.pinyin: hǎo ba wǒ yǐ hòu yào shi bān jiā yí dìng qǐng nǐ bāng máng
voice_over.translation: OK. If I move in the future, I'll definitely ask for your help.

hanzi: 天明,前边没人了,我帮你把行李搬进去吧。
pinyin: Tiānmíng qiánbian méi rén le wǒ bāng nǐ bǎ xíngli bān jìnqù ba
translation: Tianming, there's no one ahead. Let me help you move the luggage in.

scenes.scene_goal: 推动剧情发展，从对话转向行动，柯林主动提供帮助。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 柯林回头看了一眼宿舍门口，发现排队的人少了。他转过身，对张天明说，然后自然地弯腰去拎一个大行李箱。
scenes.setting_en: Daytime, in front of the entrance to a university dormitory. The queue of students has disappeared.
scenes.camera_movement: Camera follows Ke Lin as he turns to look at the dorm entrance, then tilts down as he bends to pick up a suitcase.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium shot. The American student (Ke Lin) looks towards a building entrance, then turns back to his friend. He speaks and then bends down to grab the handle of a large suitcase on the ground.

voice_over.speaker: 柯林
voice_over.hanzi: 天明,前边没人了,我帮你把行李搬进去吧。
voice_over.pinyin: Tiānmíng qiánbian méi rén le wǒ bāng nǐ bǎ xíngli bān jìnqù ba
voice_over.translation: Tianming, there's no one ahead. Let me help you move the luggage in.

hanzi: 好,谢谢。哎,我的电脑呢？……糟糕,电脑可能拉在出租车上了！
pinyin: hǎo xièxie āi wǒ de diànnǎo ne zāogāo diànnǎo kěnéng là zài chūzūchē shang le
translation: OK, thanks. Hey, where's my computer? ... Oh no! I might have left the computer in the taxi!

scenes.scene_goal: 制造剧情转折和高潮，张天明发现电脑丢失，情绪从感谢瞬间变为惊慌。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 张天明先是笑着道谢。突然，他像是想起了什么，表情一变，开始环顾四周和自己的行李，最后脸上露出惊慌失措的表情，喊出电脑不见了。
scenes.setting_en: Daytime, right at the entrance of the university dormitory, surrounded by luggage.
scenes.camera_movement: Start with a medium shot on Zhang Tianming, then a quick push-in to a close-up as his expression changes to panic.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium shot of the Asian student (Zhang Tianming). He smiles and says thanks as his friend picks up a bag. Suddenly, his expression changes to confusion, then panic. He frantically pats his backpack and looks around his luggage. The camera pushes in to a tight close-up on his panicked face.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 好,谢谢。哎,我的电脑呢？……糟糕,电脑可能拉在出租车上了！
voice_over.pinyin: hǎo xièxie. āi, wǒ de diànnǎo ne? ...zāogāo, diànnǎo kěnéng là zài chūzūchē shang le!
voice_over.translation: OK, thanks. Hey, where's my computer? ... Oh no! I might have left the computer in the taxi!

global_config.teaching_style: Clear, structured, and encouraging, with a focus on breaking down sentences and vocabulary for practical use. The style is like a friendly tutor guiding the learner step-by-step.
global_config.visual_style: Clean, modern, and card-based, suitable for template animation and dynamic text overlays.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the introduction of the main character and learn the term for 'college freshman'.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's start with the first sentence. This introduces our main character, Zhang Tianming. The sentence says [zh:张天明是大学一年级的新生]. This means 'Zhang Tianming is a college freshman.' Let's break it down: [zh:大学] means 'university,' [zh:一年级] means 'first year,' and [zh:新生] means 'new student.' So, a 'university first-year new student' is a college freshman. Pay attention to the word [zh:新生], as it's a key term for this lesson.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 张天明是大学一年级的新生。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng shì dàxué yī niánjí de xīnshēng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Zhang Tianming is a college freshman.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A term for freshmen. The opposite, a returning student, is called 老生 (lǎoshēng), literally 'old student'.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Composed of 新 (xīn) meaning 'new' and 生 (shēng) meaning 'student' or 'to be born'. It literally means 'new student'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the character '张天明' appearing, followed by text bubbles for '大学', '一年级', and '新生' combining to form 'college freshman'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to talk about the start of school and traveling a long distance.

narration.subtitle_en: The story continues: [zh:快开学了,他家离大学很远,得坐飞机去学校]. This tells us two things. First, [zh:快开学了] means 'school is about to start.' The [zh:快...了] structure indicates an imminent action. Second, [zh:他家离大学很远] means 'his home is very far from the university.' [zh:离] is a key word for distance. Because it's far, he [zh:得坐飞机去学校], 'must take a plane to go to school.' The word [zh:得] here is pronounced 'děi' and means 'must' or 'have to'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 快开学了,他家离大学很远,得坐飞机去学校。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Kuài kāixué le, tā jiā lí dàxué hěn yuǎn, děi zuò fēijī qù xuéxiào.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: School is starting soon. His home is very far from the university, so he has to take a plane to school.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb-object phrase used to describe the start of a school term.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Made of 开 (kāi), meaning 'to open' or 'to start', and 学 (xué), meaning 'to study' or 'school'. So, it literally means 'school opens'.

segments.visual_notes: Show a map with a house and a university far apart. Animate a plane flying between them.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to express the duration of an action using '了' and '多'.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, we learn about his journey: [zh:他坐飞机坐了两个多小时]. This means 'He was on the plane for more than two hours.' The structure here is 'Verb + 了 + Duration'. The particle [zh:了] after the verb indicates the completion of the action of flying for a certain time. The word [zh:多] after the number and measure word means 'more than' or 'over,' so [zh:两个多小时] is 'more than two hours.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他坐飞机坐了两个多小时,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tā zuò fēijī zuò le liǎng ge duō xiǎoshí,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: He was on the plane for more than two hours,

segments.visual_notes: Animate a clock with its hands spinning for over two hours next to an icon of a plane.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn phrases for sequential actions, like getting off a plane and hailing a taxi.

narration.subtitle_en: What happens after he lands? The text says: [zh:下飞机以后,马上叫了一辆出租汽车]. Let's break this down. [zh:下飞机] means 'to get off the plane,' and [zh:以后] means 'after.' So, 'after getting off the plane.' Then, [zh:马上] means 'immediately.' He immediately [zh:叫了一辆出租汽车], which means 'hailed a taxi.' Notice the measure word [zh:辆], which is used for vehicles.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 下飞机以后,马上叫了一辆出租汽车,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: xià fēijī yǐhòu, mǎshàng jiào le yí liàng chūzū qìchē,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: After getting off the plane, he immediately hailed a taxi,

highlight_words.explanation_en: This is a measure word specifically used for wheeled vehicles like cars, buses, and bicycles.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: The character 辆 contains the radical 车 (chē), which means 'vehicle', hinting at its usage.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a person getting off a plane, then immediately an icon of hailing a taxi.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the use of '就' to indicate a quick and expected result.

narration.subtitle_en: The final part of his journey: [zh:很快就到了学校宿舍]. This means 'and quickly arrived at the school dorm.' The phrase [zh:很快] means 'very quickly.' The word [zh:就] here connects the two actions and implies that the arrival happened smoothly and without delay after hailing the cab. Finally, [zh:到了学校宿舍] means 'arrived at the school dormitory.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 很快就到了学校宿舍。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: hěn kuài jiù dào le xuéxiào sùshè.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: and quickly arrived at the school dorm.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a taxi moving quickly across the screen and stopping at a building labeled '宿舍' (sùshè).

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to make a simple exclamation about a large number of people.

narration.subtitle_en: Upon arrival, Zhang Tianming makes an observation: [zh:人真多]! This is a simple but very common exclamation. It means 'There are so many people!' or 'It's really crowded!' Let's look at the words: [zh:人] means 'people,' [zh:真] means 'really' or 'truly,' and [zh:多] means 'many' or 'a lot.' You can use this 'Subject + [zh:真] + Adjective' pattern to express strong feelings about something.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 人真多！
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Rén zhēn duō!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: There are so many people!

segments.visual_notes: Show a background with many silhouetted figures to represent a crowd.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask a question seeking confirmation using the particle '吧'.

narration.subtitle_en: Someone notices Zhang Tianming and asks a question: [zh:你是新生吧]? This means 'You're a new student, right?' The speaker is making an assumption and looking for confirmation. The key to this is the particle [zh:吧] at the end of the sentence. It turns a statement into a soft, speculative question, similar to adding 'right?' or 'I assume?' in English.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你是新生吧？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ shì xīnshēng ba?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: You're a new student, right?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A term for freshmen. The opposite, a returning student, is called 老生 (lǎoshēng), literally 'old student'.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Composed of 新 (xīn) meaning 'new' and 生 (shēng) meaning 'student' or 'to be born'. It literally means 'new student'.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a question mark with the character '吧' inside it.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to confirm information and ask a reciprocal question using '呢'.

narration.subtitle_en: Zhang Tianming replies: [zh:是,我是新生。你呢]? This is a classic response pattern. First, he confirms with [zh:是], meaning 'Yes,' and repeats the key information: [zh:我是新生], 'I am a new student.' Then, he asks the question back using [zh:你呢]? This is a very efficient way to say 'And you?' or 'How about you?'. The particle [zh:呢] is extremely useful for asking reciprocal questions.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 是,我是新生。你呢？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shì, wǒ shì xīnshēng. Nǐ ne?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Yes, I'm a new student. And you?

segments.visual_notes: Show a checkmark for '是', then a speech bubble with '你呢?' pointing back to the other person.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the word for 'graduate student' and how to describe a helpful action.

narration.subtitle_en: The other person introduces himself: [zh:我是研究生。在这儿帮新生搬东西]. He says, [zh:我是研究生], 'I'm a graduate student.' This is a great word to know, as it contrasts with [zh:新生]. He then explains what he's doing: [zh:在这儿帮新生搬东西], which means 'Here helping new students move things.' [zh:在这儿] means 'here,' [zh:帮] means 'to help,' and [zh:搬东西] means 'to move things.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我是研究生。在这儿帮新生搬东西。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ shì yánjiūshēng. Zài zhèr bāng xīnshēng bān dōngxi.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I'm a graduate student. I'm here helping the freshmen move their things.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A student who is studying for a master's or doctoral degree.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Composed of 研究 (yánjiū) 'research' and 生 (shēng) 'student'. A 'research student'.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a student with a graduation cap, then an icon of helping another person with boxes.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the standard polite way to ask for someone's name.

narration.subtitle_en: After introducing himself, he politely asks for Zhang Tianming's name: [zh:请问,你叫什么名字]? This is one of the most important phrases for a beginner. [zh:请问] is a polite way to start a question, like 'Excuse me, may I ask...?'. The question itself is [zh:你叫什么名字]?, which literally translates to 'You are called what name?'. This is the standard way to ask 'What's your name?'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 请问,你叫什么名字？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Qǐngwèn, nǐ jiào shénme míngzi?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: May I ask, what is your name?

segments.visual_notes: Highlight '请问' as a 'polite phrase' marker.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the standard response for giving one's name.

narration.subtitle_en: Zhang Tianming gives a simple, direct answer: [zh:我叫张天明]. This means 'My name is Zhang Tianming.' The structure is very straightforward: 'I am called [Name]'. So, to give your own name, you just say [zh:我叫] followed by your name. This is the standard answer to the question [zh:你叫什么名字]?

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我叫张天明。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ jiào Zhāng Tiānmíng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: My name is Zhang Tianming.

segments.visual_notes: Show a name tag with '张天明' being written on it.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask if a name is a Chinese name.

narration.subtitle_en: The graduate student hears the name and asks a follow-up question: [zh:张天明？是中文名字吗]? First, he repeats the name, [zh:张天明]?, to confirm he heard it correctly. Then he asks, [zh:是中文名字吗]? This means, 'Is that a Chinese name?' [zh:中文] means 'Chinese language,' and [zh:名字] means 'name.' The particle [zh:吗] at the end turns the statement into a yes-no question.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 张天明？是中文名字吗？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng? Shì Zhōngwén míngzi ma?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Zhang Tianming? Is that a Chinese name?

segments.visual_notes: Show the name '张天明' with a Chinese flag icon and a question mark.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to talk about one's family origin using the '是...的' structure.

narration.subtitle_en: Zhang Tianming confirms and explains his background. He starts with [zh:对,我爸爸妈妈是从中国来的]. [zh:对] means 'Correct.' The next part, [zh:我爸爸妈妈是从中国来的], means 'My parents are from China.' This sentence uses the [zh:是...的] structure to emphasize the origin. It's not just that they came, but that the place they came *from* was China. This is a very common way to emphasize details about a past event.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 对,我爸爸妈妈是从中国来的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Duì, wǒ bàba māma shì cóng Zhōngguó lái de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Yes, my parents are from China.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a map showing an arrow from China to another location, with '爸爸妈妈' highlighted.

segments.segment_title: Born and Raised in the U.S.
segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to talk about where one was born and raised, again using the '是...的' structure.

narration.subtitle_en: He then clarifies his own situation: [zh:可是我是美国出生,在美国长大的]. [zh:可是] means 'but.' The sentence means 'But I was born and grew up in the U.S.' Notice the two key verbs: [zh:出生] means 'to be born,' and [zh:长大] means 'to grow up.' This also uses the [zh:是...的] structure to emphasize the location of these past events. The full form would be [zh:我是在美国出生的], but the first [zh:是] is often omitted.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 可是我是美国出生,在美国长大的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Kěshì wǒ shì Měiguó chūshēng, zài Měiguó zhǎngdà de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: But I was born and grew up in the U.S.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb used to state one's place or time of birth.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Composed of 出 (chū) 'to come out' and 生 (shēng) 'to be born/life'. It vividly describes the act of being born.

segments.visual_notes: Show an American flag with icons for a baby ('出生') and a growing person ('长大').

segments.teaching_goal: To learn an indirect and polite way to ask for someone's name.

narration.subtitle_en: After sharing his story, Zhang Tianming politely prompts the other person to share his name. He says, [zh:请问你的名字是]... This is a very subtle and polite way to ask. It means, 'May I ask, your name is...?' By trailing off, he invites the other person to complete the sentence. It's a slightly more indirect alternative to asking [zh:你叫什么名字]?

on_screen_text.focus_text: 请问你的名字是……
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Qǐngwèn nǐ de míngzi shì...
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: And your name is...?

segments.visual_notes: Show a speech bubble with '请问你的名字是' followed by animated dots '...'

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to state that you are currently studying something.

narration.subtitle_en: The graduate student, Ke Lin, responds: [zh:我正在学中文,我的中文名字是柯林]. The first part, [zh:我正在学中文], means 'I am studying Chinese.' The key word [zh:正在] indicates an action that is currently in progress. He then says, [zh:我的中文名字是柯林], 'My Chinese name is Ke Lin.' This is a direct and clear introduction.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我正在学中文,我的中文名字是柯林。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ zhèngzài xué Zhōngwén, wǒ de Zhōngwén míngzi shì Kē Lín.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I'm studying Chinese. My Chinese name is Ke Lin.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a book with '中文' on the cover, with '正在' highlighted to show 'in progress'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask for the specific characters that make up a Chinese name.

narration.subtitle_en: Ke Lin is curious about the characters in Zhang Tianming's name. He asks, [zh:你的名字是哪三个字]? This means, 'What are the three characters in your name?' This is a very common question in China because many characters can have the same pinyin pronunciation. Asking [zh:哪三个字] ('which three characters?') is the way to find out exactly how the name is written.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你的名字是哪三个字？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ de míngzi shì nǎ sān ge zì?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: What are the three characters in your name?

segments.visual_notes: Show three empty squares representing characters, with a question mark over them.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn two common methods for clarifying which Chinese character is being used.

narration.subtitle_en: Now, Zhang Tianming explains his name, character by character. This is a fantastic example of how Chinese people clarify characters. For his surname, he says, [zh:张是弓长张,就是一张纸的张]. He uses two methods here. First, by breaking down the character into its components: [zh:弓] 'bow' plus [zh:长] 'long' makes [zh:张]. Second, by using it in a common phrase: the [zh:张] from [zh:一张纸], which means 'a sheet of paper.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 张是弓长张,就是一张纸的张,
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhāng shì gōng cháng Zhāng, jiùshì yī zhāng zhǐ de Zhāng,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Zhang is 'gong-chang' Zhang, the 'zhang' in 'a sheet of paper',

highlight_words.explanation_en: A weapon for shooting arrows, used here as a component to describe a character.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the characters '弓' and '长' merging to form '张'. Then show an icon of a sheet of paper with '张' on it.

segments.teaching_goal: To see more examples of clarifying characters by using them in common words.

narration.subtitle_en: He continues with the rest of his name: [zh:天是天气的天,明是明天的明]. This follows the second method we just saw. To explain the character [zh:天], he says it's the 'tian' from the word [zh:天气], which means 'weather.' To explain the character [zh:明], he says it's the 'ming' from the word [zh:明天], which means 'tomorrow.' This is a very practical and common way to handle ambiguity in spoken Chinese.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 天是天气的天,明是明天的明。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: tiān shì tiānqì de tiān, míng shì míngtiān de míng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Tian is the 'tian' in 'weather', and Ming is the 'ming' in 'tomorrow'.

segments.visual_notes: Show the word '天气' with '天' highlighted, then the word '明天' with '明' highlighted.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask about the means of transportation someone used to arrive, using the '是...的' construction.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, Zhang Tianming is asked, 'How did you get to school?'. The sentence is [zh:你是怎么来学校的]. Let's break it down. [zh:你] is 'you'. [zh:是...的] is a very common structure used to ask for or emphasize details about a past event, such as the time, place, or manner. Here, it focuses on the manner, which is asked with [zh:怎么], meaning 'how'. [zh:来学校] means 'to come to school'. So, the question specifically asks about the method of your arrival.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你是怎么来学校的？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ shì zěnme lái xuéxiào de?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: How did you get to school?

highlight_words.explanation_en: An interrogative pronoun used to ask about the manner, method, or reason for something.
A grammatical structure used to emphasize the time, place, manner, or agent of a past action.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a question mark icon next to '怎么'. Highlight the '是' and '的' characters to show they form a structure.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe a sequence of travel actions and ask a follow-up question.

narration.subtitle_en: Zhang Tianming explains his journey in two steps. He says, [zh:我先坐飞机,从机场到学校坐出租汽车]. The word [zh:先] means 'first', indicating the first action: [zh:坐飞机], 'to take a plane'. The second part, [zh:从机场到学校], means 'from the airport to the school', and the action is [zh:坐出租汽车], 'to take a taxi'. He then asks Ke Lin, [zh:你也住在这儿吗], which means 'Do you also live here?'. The particle [zh:也] means 'also' or 'too'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我先坐飞机,从机场到学校坐出租汽车。柯林,你也住在这儿吗？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ xiān zuò fēijī, cóng jīchǎng dào xuéxiào zuò chūzū qìchē. Kē Lín, nǐ yě zhù zài zhèr ma?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I first flew, then took a taxi from the airport to school. Ke Lin, do you live here, too?

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb used to indicate that an action happens before another.
A common mode of long-distance transportation.
Literally 'rental car', this is the formal term for a taxi. '出租车' (chūzūchē) is also very common.

segments.visual_notes: Use icons for a plane and a taxi. Show an arrow moving from '机场' to '学校'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to state where you live and introduce the concepts of 'freshman dorm' and 'off campus'.

narration.subtitle_en: Ke Lin clarifies his living situation. He starts with [zh:不], 'No'. Then he explains, [zh:这是新生宿舍], 'This is the freshman dorm'. The word [zh:新生] means 'new student' or 'freshman'. He then states where he lives: [zh:我住在校外], 'I live off campus'. [zh:校外] literally means 'outside the school', a useful term for university life.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 不,这是新生宿舍,我住在校外。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Bù, zhè shì xīnshēng sùshè, wǒ zhù zài xiàowài.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: No, this is the freshman dorm. I live off campus.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'new student'. The opposite is '老生' (lǎoshēng), meaning 'returning student'.
A place where students live at school.
The area outside the school grounds. The opposite is '校内' (xiàonèi), on campus.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a university building with an arrow pointing away from it for '校外'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask 'why' and to ask for a preference between two options using '还是'.

narration.subtitle_en: Zhang Tianming is curious. He first says [zh:是吗], 'Is that so?' or 'Really?', showing his surprise. Then he asks [zh:你为什么住校外], 'Why do you live off campus?'. [zh:为什么] is the standard way to ask 'why'. Finally, he asks for an opinion with a choice question: [zh:你觉得住在校内好,还是住在校外好]. This 'A [zh:还是] B' structure means 'A or B?'. He's asking, 'Do you think living on campus is better, or living off campus is better?'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 是吗？你为什么住校外？你觉得住在校内好,还是住在校外好？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shì ma? Nǐ wèishéme zhù xiàowài? Nǐ juéde zhù zài xiàonèi hǎo, háishì zhù zài xiàowài hǎo?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Is that so? Why do you live off campus? Do you think it's better to live on or off campus?

highlight_words.explanation_en: The standard question word for asking for a reason.
The area within the school grounds.
Used in questions to offer a choice between two or more options.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight the 'A 还是 B' structure. Show '校内' and '校外' on opposite sides of the screen with a question mark in the middle.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to present different viewpoints and reasons for them.

narration.subtitle_en: Ke Lin starts his detailed explanation by presenting two general viewpoints. He says [zh:有的人] which means 'some people'. First, 'Some people like to live in school dorms'. Why? They [zh:觉得], or 'feel', it's [zh:又方便又安全] - 'both convenient and safe'. Then, he presents the other side: 'Some people like to live off campus', [zh:因为], 'because', off-campus housing is [zh:比较便宜], 'relatively inexpensive'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 有的人喜欢住在学校宿舍,觉得又方便又安全,有的人喜欢住在校外,因为校外的房子比较便宜。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yǒude rén xǐhuan zhù zài xuéxiào sùshè, juéde yòu fāngbiàn yòu ānquán, yǒude rén xǐhuan zhù zài xiào wài, yīnwèi xiàowài de fángzi bǐjiào piányi.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Some people like to live on campus, thinking it's both convenient and safe. Others like to live off campus, as off-campus housing is relatively inexpensive.
on_screen_text.notes: This is the first part of a longer sentence.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Easy to use or do; causing no difficulty.
Free from danger or risk.
An adverb used to soften an adjective, similar to 'rather' or 'fairly'.

segments.visual_notes: Create two columns. Column 1: '校内' with icons for convenience (clock) and safety (shield). Column 2: '校外' with an icon for money (dollar sign).

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to list multiple reasons using the '除了...以外, 还...' structure.

narration.subtitle_en: Now, Ke Lin gives his personal reasons. He uses a key structure: [zh:除了...以外, 还...], which means 'in addition to..., also...'. He says, 'I live off campus'. The first reason is [zh:除了想省点儿钱以外], 'in addition to wanting to save a little money'. [zh:省钱] means 'to save money'. The second reason is [zh:还因为住在校外自由], 'also because living off campus offers freedom'. [zh:自由] means 'free' or 'unconstrained'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我住在校外,除了想省点儿钱以外,还因为住在校外自由。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ zhù zài xiào wài, chúle xiǎng shěng diǎnr qián yǐwài, hái yīnwèi zhù zài xiào wài zìyóu.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I live off campus. In addition to wanting to save some money, I also want freedom.
on_screen_text.notes: This is the second part of a longer sentence.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A structure used to add another piece of information or reason.
A verb-object phrase meaning to economize.
Describes a state of having freedom and not being restricted.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight the '除了...以外, 还...' structure. Show icons for '省钱' (piggy bank) and '自由' (a bird flying).

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to add a final point using '再说' and express 'not necessarily' with '不见得'.

narration.subtitle_en: Ke Lin adds one final point to support his choice. He uses the word [zh:再说], which means 'on top of that' or 'besides'. It's a great connector for adding another reason. His point is: [zh:住在校内也不见得很方便], 'living on campus isn't necessarily that convenient'. The phrase [zh:不见得] is key here. It means 'not necessarily' or 'not likely', and it's used to cast doubt on a common assumption.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 再说,住在校内也不见得很方便。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zàishuō, zhù zài xiào nèi yě bújiànde hěn fāngbiàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: On top of that, living on campus isn't necessarily that convenient.
on_screen_text.notes: This is the final part of a longer sentence.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction used to introduce an additional reason or point.
Used to express doubt or that something is not a certainty.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a plus sign '+' next to '再说'. Highlight '不见得' and pair it with a shrugging emoji or icon.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express a decision for the future based on new information.

narration.subtitle_en: Convinced by Ke Lin's arguments, Zhang Tianming says, [zh:真的吗], 'Really?'. This shows he's taking the information on board. He then makes a decision for the future: [zh:那我以后也搬到校外去]. The word [zh:那] here acts like 'then' or 'in that case'. [zh:以后] means 'in the future'. The action is [zh:搬到校外去], 'move off campus'. The particle [zh:也] shows he wants to do the same as Ke Lin.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 真的吗？那我以后也搬到校外去。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhēn de ma? Nà wǒ yǐhòu yě bān dào xiàowài qù.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Really? Then I'll move off campus in the future, too.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common phrase to express surprise or seek confirmation.
A time word indicating a point in time after the present.
Refers to moving objects or one's residence.

segments.visual_notes: Show a lightbulb icon appearing over Zhang Tianming's head. Animate an arrow pointing from 'now' to 'future' for '以后'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to give advice using '对...有好处' and '可以'.

narration.subtitle_en: Ke Lin offers some sensible advice. He starts with [zh:你刚来], 'You've just arrived'. He then says, [zh:在学校住对你有好处], 'living at school is good for you'. The structure [zh:对...有好处] means 'is beneficial for...'. The benefit is that you [zh:可以适应一下学校的生活], 'can get used to school life'. [zh:可以] means 'can' or 'may', and [zh:适应] means 'to adapt' or 'get used to'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你刚来,在学校住对你有好处,可以适应一下学校的生活。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ gāng lái, zài xuéxiào zhù duì nǐ yǒu hǎochu, kěyǐ shìyìng yíxià xuéxiào de shēnghuó.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: You've just arrived. It's good for you to live on campus to get used to school life.
on_screen_text.notes: This is the first part of Ke Lin's advice.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb indicating that an action took place a very short time ago.
The opposite is '坏处' (huàichu), meaning disadvantage.

highlight_words.translation: to adapt, to get used to
highlight_words.explanation_en: To adjust oneself to new conditions.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight the '对...有好处' structure. Show a thumbs-up icon next to '好处'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to make a conditional offer of help using '要是'.

narration.subtitle_en: Ke Lin continues with a kind offer. He uses the word [zh:要是], which is a common way to say 'if'. The condition is: [zh:你以后想搬家], 'if you want to move in the future'. [zh:搬家] specifically means 'to move house'. The offer is: [zh:我可以帮你找房子], 'I can help you find a place'. [zh:帮] means 'to help', and [zh:找房子] means 'to look for a house or apartment'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 要是你以后想搬家,我可以帮你找房子。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yàoshi nǐ yǐhòu xiǎng bānjiā, wǒ kěyǐ bāng nǐ zhǎo fángzi.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: If you want to move later, I can help you find a place.
on_screen_text.notes: This is the second part of Ke Lin's advice.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A conjunction used to introduce a conditional clause, similar to '如果' (rúguǒ).
A verb-object phrase. '搬' means to move, and '家' means home.
A common verb for searching or seeking.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight '要是' as the 'if' part of the sentence. Show an icon of a person helping another look at a house.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to politely accept an offer of help and confirm future action.

narration.subtitle_en: Zhang Tianming accepts the offer graciously. He starts with [zh:好吧], which is a soft agreement, like 'Alright' or 'OK'. Then he repeats the condition: [zh:我以后要是搬家], 'If I move in the future'. He confirms he will take up the offer by saying [zh:一定请你帮忙]. [zh:一定] means 'definitely' or 'certainly', adding emphasis. [zh:请你帮忙] is a polite way to say 'ask for your help'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 好吧,我以后要是搬家,一定请你帮忙。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hǎo ba, wǒ yǐhòu yàoshi bānjiā, yídìng qǐng nǐ bāngmáng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: OK. If I move in the future, I'll definitely ask for your help.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A phrase used to show agreement or acceptance, often with a slight tone of resignation or consideration.
An adverb used to express certainty.

highlight_words.translation: to help, to do a favor
highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb-object phrase that means 'to lend a hand'.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight '一定' and show a checkmark icon to represent certainty.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to point out something and offer to help with a physical task.

narration.subtitle_en: Seeing an opportunity to help, Ke Lin says, [zh:前边没人了], 'There's no one ahead'. [zh:前边] means 'in front'. The phrase [zh:没人了] indicates the line has cleared. He then makes an offer using the [zh:吧] particle, which softens the suggestion: [zh:我帮你把行李搬进去吧]. This means, 'Let me help you move the luggage in'. [zh:行李] is 'luggage', and [zh:搬进去] means 'to move inside'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 天明,前边没人了,我帮你把行李搬进去吧。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tiānmíng, qiánbian méi rén le, wǒ bāng nǐ bǎ xíngli bān jìnqù ba.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Tianming, there's no one ahead. Let me help you move the luggage in.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A location word indicating the area ahead.
Bags and suitcases for travel.
A resultative complement indicating the action of moving something into a place.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a line of people disappearing. Then show an icon of someone helping another with a suitcase.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn phrases for expressing thanks, confusion, and panic.

narration.subtitle_en: This line shows a rapid change of emotion. First, Zhang Tianming agrees: [zh:好, 谢谢], 'OK, thanks'. Then, confusion hits. He says [zh:哎], an interjection like 'Hey' or 'Oh', followed by [zh:我的电脑呢], 'Where's my computer?'. The [zh:呢] particle here turns the phrase into a 'where is...?' question. Finally, panic sets in with [zh:糟糕], 'Oh no!' or 'This is terrible!'. He realizes, [zh:电脑可能拉在出租车上了], 'I might have left the computer in the taxi!'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 好,谢谢。哎,我的电脑呢？……糟糕,电脑可能拉在出租车上了！
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hǎo, xièxie. Āi, wǒ de diànnǎo ne? ...Zāogāo, diànnǎo kěnéng là zài chūzūchē shàng le!
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: OK, thanks. Hey, where's my computer? ... Oh no! I might have left the computer in the taxi!

highlight_words.explanation_en: An interjection used to express surprise or to get someone's attention.
An exclamation used when something bad has happened.
An adverb expressing possibility.
A colloquial verb meaning to forget and leave something somewhere.

segments.visual_notes: Use emojis to show the progression: 😊 → 🤔 → 😱. Highlight the word '糟糕' with a flashing red effect.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the colloquial usage and pronunciation of '拉' (là) to mean 'leave something behind'.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's focus on the word [zh:拉]. This character has two common pronunciations with different meanings. When pronounced [zh:lā] in the first tone, it means 'to pull'. However, in this dialogue, it's pronounced [zh:là] in the fourth tone. In this colloquial usage, [zh:là] means 'to leave something behind' unintentionally. So when Zhang Tianming says he [zh:拉在出租车上了], he means he accidentally left his computer in the taxi. This is a very common and useful word in daily conversation.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 拉 (là) / 拉 (lā)
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: là / lā
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: to leave behind / to pull
on_screen_text.notes: Pay attention to the different tones and meanings.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A colloquial usage. Example: 我把钥匙拉在家里了 (Wǒ bǎ yàoshi là zài jiāli le) - I left my keys at home.
The more standard meaning. Example: 拉门 (lā mén) - to pull a door.

segments.visual_notes: Show two columns. One for 'là' with an icon of a forgotten item. The other for 'lā' with an icon of someone pulling a rope.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the different uses of the particle 了 to indicate completed actions, consecutive actions, and new situations.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's dive into our first grammar point: the versatile particle [zh:了]. It often indicates that an action has been completed. In the dialogue, we heard [zh:他坐飞机坐了两个多小时], meaning he was on the plane for over two hours. Notice [zh:了] comes after the verb. It's also used when two actions happen one after another. For example, [zh:下飞机以后, 马上叫了一辆出租汽车] means 'After getting off the plane, he immediately hailed a taxi.' The first action, getting off the plane, is completed, allowing the second to happen. Finally, [zh:了] at the end of a sentence can signal a new situation, like in [zh:快开学了], 'School is starting soon.' This indicates a change is about to happen.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他坐飞机坐了两个多小时。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: tā zuò fēijī zuò le liǎng ge duō xiǎoshí.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: He was on the plane for more than two hours.
on_screen_text.notes: 了 can indicate a completed action, a new situation, or consecutive actions.

grammar_points.pattern: Verb + 了 + (Quantified) Object
grammar_points.explanation_en: Indicates a completed action, often with a quantified object. Example: 叫了一辆出租汽车 (hailed a taxi).

grammar_points.pattern: Verb Phrase 1 + 了, ... (就) + Verb Phrase 2
grammar_points.explanation_en: Indicates two consecutive actions, where the second happens after the first is completed.

grammar_points.pattern: Sentence + 了
grammar_points.explanation_en: Indicates a new situation or a change of state. Example: 快开学了 (School is starting soon).

segments.visual_notes: Animate the character '了' appearing after the verb in the example sentence. For consecutive actions, show a '1 -> 2' flow diagram.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to use the 是...的 construction to ask about and emphasize the specifics (time, place, manner) of a past event.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, let's look at the [zh:是...的] construction. We use this pattern when we already know an event happened, but we want to focus on the details, like *when*, *where*, or *how*. In the dialogue, Zhang Tianming says [zh:我爸爸妈妈是从中国来的], emphasizing that the place his parents came *from* was China. He also says [zh:我是美国出生], emphasizing the place of his birth. Notice that in positive statements, the [zh:是] is often optional. This pattern is also very common in questions asking for details, like Ke Lin's question, [zh:你是怎么来学校的?], 'How did you get to school?' He knows Zhang Tianming arrived, but he wants to know the manner of his arrival.

on_screen_text.focus_text: Q: 你是怎么来学校的？ A: 我(是)坐飞机来的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Q: nǐ shì zěnme lái xuéxiào de? A: wǒ (shì) zuò fēijī lái de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Q: How did you get to school? A: I came by plane.
on_screen_text.notes: Use 是...的 to emphasize the time, place, or manner of a known past action. 是 is often optional in affirmative sentences.

grammar_points.pattern: (是) + [Detail] + Verb + 的
grammar_points.explanation_en: Emphasizes the detail (time, place, manner, etc.) of a past action.

grammar_points.pattern: 我爸爸妈妈是从中国来的。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: wǒ bàba māmā shì cóng zhōngguó lái de.
grammar_points.explanation_en: Emphasizes *where* they came from (from China).

grammar_points.pattern: 我是美国出生, 在美国长大的。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: wǒ shì měiguó chūshēng, zài měiguó zhǎngdà de.
grammar_points.explanation_en: Emphasizes *where* he was born and grew up (in the U.S.).

segments.visual_notes: In the Q&A example, highlight the question word '怎么' and the corresponding detail '坐飞机' in the answer.

segments.teaching_goal: To differentiate between '除了...以外, 还/也...' (in addition to) and '除了...以外, 都...' (except for).

narration.subtitle_en: Now let's examine the structure [zh:除了...以外]. It has two main uses depending on what follows. The first is 'in addition to'. In the text, Ke Lin says [zh:除了想省点儿钱以外, 还因为住在校外自由]. This means 'In addition to wanting to save some money, it's also because living off-campus is freeing.' He has two reasons. The second usage means 'except for'. If the sentence uses [zh:都] instead of [zh:还] or [zh:也], it creates an exception. For example, [zh:除了他以外, 我们都去了] means 'Except for him, we all went.' So, pay close attention to whether you see [zh:还] for addition or [zh:都] for exclusion.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 除了想省点儿钱以外, 还因为住在校外自由。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: chúle xiǎng shěng diǎnr qián yǐwài, hái yīnwèi zhù zài xiàowài zìyóu.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: In addition to wanting to save some money, it's also because living off-campus offers freedom.
on_screen_text.notes: This structure adds another reason for living off-campus.

grammar_points.pattern: 除了 A 以外, 还/也 B
grammar_points.explanation_en: Meaning: In addition to A, also B. (Both A and B are true).

grammar_points.pattern: 除了 A 以外, 都 B
grammar_points.explanation_en: Meaning: Except for A, all are B. (A is the only exception).

segments.visual_notes: Show two boxes, A and B. For the '还' structure, put a plus sign between them. For the '都' structure, show a large circle containing B, with A outside the circle.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to use connecting words like 因为, 除了...以外, and 再说 to structure a multi-point explanation.

narration.subtitle_en: Our final grammar point is about connecting sentences to build a logical argument. When you have multiple reasons for something, using connecting words makes your speech clearer. Ke Lin gives a great example when explaining why he lives off-campus. He uses a series of connectors: [zh:因为]... [zh:除了...以外]... [zh:还因为]... and [zh:再说]. This sequence translates to 'because... in addition to... also because... on top of that...'. It creates a very organized and persuasive explanation. Another common way to list reasons is by using ordinal numbers: [zh:第一] (firstly), [zh:第二] (secondly), and so on. Both methods are excellent for structuring your thoughts in Chinese.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 因为..., 除了...以外, 还因为..., 再说...
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: yīnwèi..., chúle...yǐwài, hái yīnwèi..., zàishuō...
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: because..., in addition to..., also because..., on top of that...
on_screen_text.notes: Use these connectors to list multiple reasons in a clear, organized way.

grammar_points.pattern: 我住在校外, [除了]想省钱[以外], [还因为]自由。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: wǒ zhù zài xiàowài, chúle xiǎng shěng qián yǐwài, hái yīnwèi zìyóu.
grammar_points.explanation_en: A sequence of connectors used to present multiple reasons.

grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: dì yī..., dì èr..., dì sān...
grammar_points.explanation_en: An alternative way to list reasons using ordinal numbers: 'firstly..., secondly..., thirdly...'.

segments.visual_notes: Display the connecting words as links in a chain, visually connecting different reason clauses.

segments.teaching_goal: To review and reinforce the key vocabulary and grammar patterns from the lesson.

narration.subtitle_en: Excellent work! Let's quickly review the most important points from this lesson. Try to use these words and patterns in your own sentences. See you next time!

on_screen_text.focus_text: Key Phrases Review

highlight_words.translation: to live on campus / off campus
structure to emphasize details of a past action

production_notes.remarks: This schema is model-agnostic and designed for script planning plus downstream rendering.
